coconut+banana.note: Excellent shade + nitrogen synergy. Banana creates microclimate for young coconut.

coconut+black pepper.note: Pepper uses coconut trunk as standard. Coconut provides perfect dappled shade.

coconut+vanilla.note: Vanilla thrives in the high humidity environment created by coconut canopy.

arecanut+cocoa.note: Cocoa performs excellently under the light shade of arecanut.

teak+ginger.note: Teak leaf fall can suppress ginger growth if not cleared regularly.

tomato+fennel.note: Fennel stunts tomato growth through allelopathy (chemical inhibition).

coffee+black pepper.note: Shared susceptibility to root-knot nematodes. Monitor soil health carefully.

banana+cardamom.note: Banana bunchy top virus vectors can sometimes affect cardamom vigor.

papaya+black pepper.note: Both highly susceptible to Phytophthora foot rot in waterlogged soils.

mango+cashew.note: Both attract the Tea Mosquito Bug. Avoid planting in adjacent blocks without trap crops.

mango+papaya.note: Papaya grows too fast and can crowd out young mango grafts.

silver oak+coffee.note: Classic combination. Silver oak provides deep-rooted nutrient cycling without competing with coffee.

jackfruit+black pepper.note: Jackfruit provides an excellent, sturdy standard for pepper vines.

turmeric+ginger.note: Planting both together increases risk of rhizome rot spreading between them.
